title: Tripion

{{Quote|You'll be fed soon enough!|A guard to a tripion in a cage|<ref name="BF H" />}}
The '''tripion''' was a [[Crustacean/Legends|crustaceous]] [[Species/Legends|species]] with three venomous tail stingers and over 600 different subspecies. They were best suited to live in the [[Desert/Legends|deserts]] of hot [[Planet/Legends|worlds]], including the [[Moon/Legends|moons]] [[Gall/Legends|Gall]] and [[Yavin 13/Legends|Yavin 13]], with the species' [[Diet/Legends|diet]] consisting of small [[Lizard/Legends|lizards]] and [[Insect/Legends|insects]].

==Biology and appearance==
There were more than six hundred different [[Species/Legends|subspecies]] of the [[Crustacean/Legends|crustaceous]] tripion discovered, which ranged in size from less than one [[Meter/Legends|meter]] up to eighteen meters tall. Tripions had [[Limb/Legends|pincers]], [[Spider/Legends|spider]]-like legs, and generally three venomous stinger-tipped tails. Based on the subspecies, the sting could be simply annoying, or even [[Death/Legends|deadly]].<ref name="GG2 Y&B" /> One subspecies of tripion, found on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Thrantin]], had six spider-like legs, but no pincers.<ref name="AIR">''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]''</ref> Another subspecies, found on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Gall/Legends|Gall]], had four legs, two [[Antenna/Legends|antennae]], two pincers, stood upright, and had only one tail.<ref name="EGtP&M">''[[The Essential Guide to Planets and Moons]]''</ref>

==Behavior==
Although tripions could withstand high amounts of heat<ref name="GG2 Y&B" /> on moons like Gall<ref name="EGtP&M" /><ref name="BF H">''[[Boba Fett: Hunted]]''</ref> and [[Yavin 13/Legends|Yavin 13]], they would spend the [[Standard day/Legends|day]] under stones, and would come out at night. They hunted [[Insect/Legends|insects]] and small [[Lizard/Legends|lizards]], and could sting with their tails three times in rapid succession, but would only do so if the prey was struggling, or if the prey was large.<ref name="GG2 Y&B" />

==Tripions in the galaxy==
[[File:Twilight_Class_expedition.png|thumb|left|250px|Members of Twilight Class attacked by a tripion]]
When the young [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] was on the [[Desert/Legends|desert]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] to find work from the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt]] in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]],<ref name="TERC">The events of ''Boba Fett: Hunted'' took place in 22 BBY, according to ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''.</ref> Fett saw a tripion from Gall in a cage on [[Khetanna/Legends|Jabba's sail barge]]. The tripion slammed its [[Poison/Legends|poisonous]] tail against the cage and clicked its pincers, and a guard poked it with his [[Sword/Legends|sword]] for his and his comrades' entertainment.<ref name="BF H" />

When a group of trainees from the [[Galactic Outdoor Survival School/Legends|Galactic Outdoor Survival School]] was partaking in their final test on the planet Thrantin, one member of [[Twilight Class]], [[Xenon Nnaksta]], was attacked by a tripion. He was saved by another member of Twilight Class, [[Derembus Sitnalta]], who in turn sustained a bad, but non-lethal, sting in doing so.<ref name="AIR" />

==Behind the scenes==
Tripions were first mentioned in the [[1989]] sourcebook ''[[Galaxy Guide 2: Yavin and Bespin]]'', which was published for use with [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="GG2 Y&B" /> Their first depiction was in the [[1995]] West End Games roleplaying sourcebook ''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]''.<ref name="AIR" /> The [[1998]] reference book ''[[The Essential Guide to Planets and Moons]]'', authored by [[Daniel Wallace]], referred to tripions as "Gallian tripions."<ref name="EGtP&M" /> The tripions first appeared in the [[2004]] young-reader novel ''[[Boba Fett: Hunted]]'', written by [[Elizabeth Hand]] as the fourth installment of the [[Star Wars: Boba Fett (novel series)|''Boba Fett'']] series. In the book they were referred to as "Gallion tripions" and possessed one stinger tipped tail.<ref name="BF H" />
